Title: ERF.D2 negatively controls drought tolerance through synergistic regulation of abscisic acid and jasmonic acid in tomato

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes drought-induced jasmonic acid accumulation indirectly by phosphorylating ERF.D2 and facilitating its degradation, thereby enhancing tomato drought tolerance.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPK27 directly interacts with ERF.D2 and this interaction is enhanced by ABA. 2. CPK27 phosphorylates ERF.D2 mainly at Ser-52 in an ABA-dependent manner. 3. CPK27-mediated phosphorylation promotes PUB22-dependent ubiquitination and proteasomal degradation of ERF.D2, relieving repression of AOC and OPR3 and increasing JA accumulation under drought.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; pull-down; LCI; co-IP; in vitro phosphorylation assay; Phos-tag assay; LC-MS/MS phosphosite identification; cell-free degradation assay; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; genetic analysis of cpk27 and cpk27 erf.d2
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: drought stress; ABA treatment; mannitol-induced osmotic stress; MG132; CHX
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts upstream of ERF.D2 in ABA signaling. The metabolic effect on JA is indirect through post-translational control of the ERF transcriptional repressor.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving drought tolerance by enhancing ABA-triggered ERF.D2 turnover and thereby increasing stress-induced JA biosynthesis.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes drought-induced jasmonic acid accumulation indirectly by ubiquitinating phosphorylated ERF.D2 and promoting its degradation, thereby enhancing drought tolerance.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PUB22 is an ABA-enhanced interactor of ERF.D2 identified by IP-MS and confirmed by multiple interaction assays. 2. PUB22 directly ubiquitinates ERF.D2 in vitro and in vivo. 3. PUB22 preferentially ubiquitinates phosphorylated/phosphomimic ERF.D2, promoting its degradation and thereby enabling AOC/OPR3 expression and JA accumulation during drought.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: IP-MS; Y2H/LCI/co-IP as cited in supplementary figures; in vitro ubiquitination assay; in vivo ubiquitination assay; mutant analysis of pub22 and pub22 erf.d2
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts downstream or in concert with ABA/CPK27-mediated phosphorylation to target ERF.D2 for 26S proteasome degradation. The effect on JA is indirect through turnover of a transcriptional repressor.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance stress-resilient JA induction by strengthening degradation of ERF.D2 under drought.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: pub22 mutants are drought sensitive; broader ubiquitination targets and pleiotropic effects not resolved.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediate leading to target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes drought-induced JA accumulation and contributes positively to drought tolerance; its expression is repressed by ERF.D2 and derepressed in erf.d2 mutants.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOC is one of the most strongly induced JA biosynthesis genes in drought-treated erf.d2 mutants. 2. ERF.D2 directly binds the AOC promoter GCC motif and represses its transcription. 3. Silencing AOC in erf.d2 plants reduces JA accumulation and suppresses the enhanced drought tolerance phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; Y1H; dual-LUC; VIGS; JA quantification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: drought stress; ABA acts indirectly via ERF.D2 degradation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by drought; expression is higher in erf.d2 mutants than WT under drought.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native AOC promoter; inducible by drought
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Jasmonate biosynthetic enzyme, allene oxide cyclase family
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; identified as a dominant drought-responsive JA biosynthesis gene in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering target to raise drought-induced JA biosynthesis, potentially combined with ERF.D2 suppression.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct target of ERF.D2 repression through GCC motif-containing promoter region.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate downstream precursor en route to target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes drought-induced JA accumulation and contributes positively to drought tolerance; its expression is directly repressed by ERF.D2 and elevated in erf.d2 mutants.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OPR3 is strongly induced by drought and shows higher transcript accumulation in erf.d2 mutants than WT. 2. ERF.D2 directly binds the OPR3 promoter GCC motif and represses its transcription. 3. Silencing OPR3 in erf.d2 plants decreases JA accumulation and compromises the enhanced drought tolerance phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; Y1H; dual-LUC; VIGS; JA quantification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: drought stress; ABA acts indirectly via ERF.D2 degradation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by drought; expression is higher in erf.d2 mutants than WT under drought.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native OPR3 promoter; inducible by drought
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; identified as a dominant drought-responsive JA biosynthesis gene in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for boosting JA-dependent drought tolerance, especially with derepression via ERF.D2 editing.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct ERF.D2 target; promoter binding depends on AP2-domain residues including Lys-115, Arg-117, Arg-132, Arg-166, and Lys-168.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF family transcription factor with AP2 DNA-binding domain; contains EAR-like LxL repression motif
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression; protein destabilization through ABA-dependent phosphorylation and PUB22-mediated ubiquitination
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses AOC and OPR3 transcription by binding GCC motifs in their promoters.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates drought-induced JA biosynthesis and drought tolerance; loss of ERF.D2 increases AOC/OPR3 expression and JA accumulation, while overexpression decreases drought tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent negative controller that links ABA signaling to JA biosynthesis by acting as a repressing hub removed during drought.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ERF.D2 is a nuclear AP2/ERF transcriptional repressor that negatively regulates tomato drought tolerance. 2. ERF.D2 directly binds AOC and OPR3 promoters and represses their expression, thereby reducing JA accumulation. 3. ABA activates CPK27 and PUB22 to phosphorylate, ubiquitinate, and degrade ERF.D2, relieving repression of JA biosynthesis under drought.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; overexpression; drought phenotyping; ABA stomatal assay; Y2H; pull-down; LCI; co-IP; Phos-tag; LC-MS/MS; ubiquitination assays; RNA-seq; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; Y1H; dual-LUC; VIGS rescue/suppression assays; JA quantification
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: drought stress; ABA treatment; mannitol; MG132; CHX
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript induced by drought, ABA, and mannitol; protein abundance decreases under drought, ABA, and mannitol due to proteasomal degradation.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: drought; ABA; osmotic stress; CPK27-mediated phosphorylation; PUB22-mediated ubiquitination
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely involved in negative feedback regulation, as its transcript is induced by drought/ABA while its protein is degraded under stress.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No stable named protein complex established; directly interacts with CPK27 and PUB22.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for genome editing or knockdown to enhance drought tolerance by increasing endogenous JA biosynthesis without exogenous hormone application.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes drought sensitivity; broader growth or stress tradeoffs were not reported.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ser-52 is the major CPK27 phosphorylation site controlling stability. Binding to AOC/OPR3 promoters is attenuated by drought. Five AP2-domain residues contribute to promoter binding, with Arg-132 critical for AOC repression. Exogenous ABA cannot rescue OE-ERF.D2 drought sensitivity, supporting ERF.D2 action downstream of ABA signaling.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF family transcription factor with AP2 DNA-binding domain; contains EAR-like LxL repression motif
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA acts upstream in drought signaling; JA promotes drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: integration of ABA signaling with JA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: acts downstream of ABA signaling and represses JA biosynthetic gene expression until degraded
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses AOC and OPR3; indirectly suppresses JA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Integrates ABA-triggered post-translational regulation with JA biosynthesis to determine drought tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central hub at the ABA-JA intersection in drought response.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABA promotes ERF.D2 degradation through CPK27 and PUB22. 2. ERF.D2 acts downstream of canonical ABA components in controlling drought response. 3. Removal of ERF.D2 repression permits JA biosynthesis and drought tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ABA treatment; VIGS of PYL7/CYP707/SnRK2.6 in erf.d2; double mutants cpk27 erf.d2 and pub22 erf.d2; JA measurement
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript induced by ABA/drought but protein destabilized by ABA/drought.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing ERF.D2 could exploit endogenous ABA-JA crosstalk to improve drought resilience.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This second entry captures the integrator role across two hormones explicitly emphasized by the paper.
